title: Life of Saint Cecilia: six comparative streams
summary: CME00145 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic transcription of printed pp. 72-102 of Bertha Ellen Lovewell's 1898 comparative edition of Saint Cecilia traditions. The encoded selection is not one medieval work: its six streams represent four works, with three witness versions of one South English Legendary life, one Latin Legenda Aurea life, one expanded Northern Homily Cycle life, and one Scottish Legendary life. Lovewell's Ashmole stream depends on Furnivall's printing; her new Bodley 779 and Cotton Cleopatra D IX streams came through unidentified library-made working transcripts; Laud Misc. 108 and Trinity R.3.25 supply apparatus readings; the Scottish stream depends on Horstmann and Metcalfe; and Lovewell's pp. 92-102 directly transcribe Horstmann's complete Harley/Tiberius edited Northern text rather than the fire-damaged Tiberius witness alone. Complete public digital objects are cached for Ashmole 43, Bodley 779, Laud Misc. 108, Trinity R.3.25, and Yale Takamiya MS 54, each with the Cecilia locus mapped inside the whole manuscript. Official catalogs, request routes, and locally cached catalog evidence are also recorded for collateral South English Legendary witnesses.
review_notes: The abstract South English Legendary, expanded Northern Homily Cycle, Scottish Legendary, and Legenda Aurea works are modeled separately from their manuscript witnesses, printed editions, digital encodings, and repository artifact.

Lovewell's printed heading 'Bodley 799' is corrected to current shelfmark Bodley 779. Her Scottish heading's '364a-358b' is contradicted by its own internal milestones and the Cambridge catalog; the current locus is ff. 354r-360v.

The Tiberius-labeled stream contains internal milestones for ff. 192 and 193, the Harley 4196 locus. Tiberius itself is fire-damaged and incomplete at ff. 271rb-273va; Horstmann prints a complete H/T reconstructed text with H and T apparatus.

The current British Library holding record describes the Northern Homily Cycle broadly at ff. 101r-281r; DIMEV v1.0 supplies the exact Cecilia locus ff. 271rb-273va, while Horstmann's historical heading gives ff. 271-274b. These layers are recorded without treating the holding catalog as exact-locus evidence.
Yale's IIIF object has 404 canvases, but its downloadable PDF has 405 pages because page 1 is a generated metadata sheet. Cecilia canvases 337-344 therefore map to PDF pages 338-345.

Lovewell's statement that Stowe 946 was omitted conflicts with the current DIMEV census, which identifies the relevant South English Legendary witness as Stowe 949. The omitted witness is not treated as an edition source.

Local research caching does not itself grant redistribution or commercial print-on-demand permission. Image rights and provider terms remain separate from the public-domain medieval texts and nineteenth-century edition text.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00145
entities.description: The exact XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00145; the file is 194,511 bytes.
The XML has two DIV1 elements, six version DIV2 elements, 1,856 L, ten P, 340 NOTE, 30 MILESTONE, and 63 PB elements.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00145
entities.description: A public electronic transcription of Lovewell's title page and printed pp. 72-102, retaining six continuous version streams, apparatus, pagination, italics, and manuscript milestones while omitting the introduction, grammar, metrics, notes, glossaries, and bibliography.

entities.label: Bertha Ellen Lovewell, The Life of St. Cecilia (1898)
entities.description: A Yale Studies in English monograph presenting comparative historical introduction, six continuous Cecilia versions on pp. 72-102, textual variants, notes, glossaries, and bibliography. CME00145 represents only the title page and pp. 72-102.

bibliographic.citation: Bertha Ellen Lovewell, The Life of St. Cecilia from MS. Ashmole 43 and MS. Cotton Tiberius E. VII: With Introduction, Variants, and Glossary, Yale Studies in English 3, ed. Albert S. Cook (Boston, New York, and London: Lamson, Wolffe and Company, 1898).
bibliographic.title: The Life of St. Cecilia from MS. Ashmole 43 and MS. Cotton Tiberius E. VII

notes: The preface is dated New Haven, 1 June 1898.
Lovewell says newly printed texts were carefully transcribed from originals and collated by scribes at the respective libraries, without naming those scribes.

entities.label: Life of Saint Cecilia in the South English Legendary
entities.description: The anonymous Middle English couplet life indexed by DIMEV record 4572 / IMEV and NIMEV 2873. Lovewell represents Ashmole 43 and variants, Bodley 779, and Cotton Cleopatra D IX as three continuous streams, with Laud Misc. 108 and Trinity R.3.25 in the Ashmole apparatus.

entities.label: De sancta Caecilia in the Legenda Aurea
entities.description: Jacobus de Voragine's Latin prose life of Saint Cecilia, printed by Lovewell in full as an independent comparison rather than identified as the direct exemplar of any one English stream.

entities.label: Life of Saint Cecilia in the expanded Northern Homily Cycle
entities.description: An anonymous Middle English couplet life indexed by DIMEV record 2803 / IMEV and NIMEV 1671, surviving in fire-damaged Cotton Tiberius E VII and complete Harley 4196.

entities.label: Cecile in the Scottish Legendary
entities.description: An anonymous Older Scots couplet life preserved uniquely in Cambridge University Library MS Gg.2.6. The older attribution to John Barbour used by Lovewell and Horstmann is not retained as authorship.

entities.label: F. J. Furnivall, Originals and Analogues, Part II (1875)
entities.description: The Chaucer Society printing from which Lovewell says her Ashmole punctuation and pointing were taken; DIMEV locates the Ashmole Cecilia text at pp. 208-218. The available Internet Archive item is a later bound installment beginning after the target and therefore does not reproduce those pages.

bibliographic.citation: F. J. Furnivall, ed., Originals and Analogues of Some of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, Part II, Chaucer Society, Second Series 10 (London: Trübner, 1875), Life of Saint Cecilia, 208-218.
bibliographic.title: Originals and Analogues of Some of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, Part II

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Zu Chaucer's Caecilien-Legende (1877)
entities.description: Kölbing's study prints the complete Harley 4196 Northern Cecilia text under the manuscript rubric De sancta Caecilia historia on pp. 235-240. Lovewell cites the printing while using Horstmann's later H/T presentation for her text and punctuation.

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, 'Zu Chaucer's Caecilien-Legende,' Englische Studien 1 (1877): 215-248; Harley Cecilia text 235-240.
bibliographic.title: Zu Chaucer's Caecilien-Legende

entities.label: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden, Neue Folge (1881)
entities.description: Horstmann's complete edition of the expanded Northern Homily Cycle Cecilia on pp. 159-164, headed from Tiberius ff. 271-274b but constructed with H and T readings. Its apparatus explicitly distinguishes H and T, while the continuous text remains complete despite Tiberius fire loss. Lovewell's Northern stream on pp. 92-102 directly reproduces this edited text, subject to editorial punctuation and presentation.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden, Neue Folge (Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1881), 'De sancta Cecilia historia,' 159-164.
bibliographic.title: Altenglische Legenden, Neue Folge

entities.label: Carl Horstmann, Barbour's Legendensammlung, volume 2 (1882)
entities.description: Horstmann's edition of the Scottish Legendary from CUL Gg.2.6; Cecilia is printed on pp. 164-172 and was one of Lovewell's authorities for punctuation and capitalization.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., Barbour's des schottischen Nationaldichters Legendensammlung, vol. 2 (Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1882), Cecilia, 164-172.
bibliographic.title: Barbour's des schottischen Nationaldichters Legendensammlung

entities.label: W. M. Metcalfe, Legends of the Saints, volume 2 (1890)
entities.description: The Scottish Text Society edition from the unique CUL Gg.2.6 manuscript. Cecilia is printed on pp. 368-386 and was used with Horstmann by Lovewell for punctuation and capitalization.

bibliographic.citation: W. M. Metcalfe, ed., Legends of the Saints in the Scottish Dialect of the Fourteenth Century, vol. 2, Scottish Text Society (Edinburgh and London: William Blackwood and Sons, 1890), Cecilia, 368-386.
bibliographic.title: Legends of the Saints in the Scottish Dialect of the Fourteenth Century

entities.label: Theodor Grässe, Legenda Aurea (1846)
entities.description: The first Grässe edition cited in Lovewell's bibliography; chapter CLXIX, De sancta Caecilia, occupies pp. 771-776.

bibliographic.citation: Jacobus de Voragine, Legenda aurea, ed. Theodor Grässe (Leipzig: Arnold, 1846), chap. CLXIX, 771-776.
bibliographic.title: Legenda aurea

entities.label: Theodor Grässe, Legenda Aurea, second edition (1850)
entities.description: The second Grässe edition cited in Lovewell's bibliography; chapter CLXIX, De sancta Caecilia, occupies pp. 771-776. The exact issue copied by Lovewell has not been established.

bibliographic.citation: Jacobus de Voragine, Legenda aurea, 2nd ed., ed. Theodor Grässe (Leipzig: Arnold, 1850), chap. CLXIX, 771-776.
bibliographic.title: Legenda aurea

notes: Second edition.

entities.label: Library-made working transcript of Bodley 779 Cecilia
entities.description: The unidentified scholarly transcript from which Lovewell says she copied the newly printed Bodley text after it was transcribed and collated at the holding library.
notes: No named scribe, current repository, or surviving copy was established.

entities.label: Library-made working transcript of Cotton Cleopatra D IX Cecilia
entities.description: The unidentified scholarly transcript from which Lovewell says she copied the newly printed Cleopatra text after it was transcribed and collated at the holding library.
notes: No named scribe, current repository, or surviving copy was established.

entities.label: Working collation of Laud Misc. 108 and Trinity R.3.25 Cecilia readings
entities.description: The unnamed library-supplied comparison underlying the L and C apparatus sigla around Lovewell's Ashmole central text.
notes: Lovewell's general preface supports library collation, but the exact persons, sheets, and survival history are unknown.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Ashmole 43
entities.description: Lovewell's South English Legendary central witness; its Life of Saint Cecilia occupies ff. 185v-188v.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: First quarter of the fourteenth century

physical_description.extent: x + 265 + xi leaves; Cecilia at ff. 185v-188v
notes: The catalog localizes the scribe's orthography to Gloucestershire, eLALME LP 7170.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 779
entities.description: A second-quarter-fifteenth-century South English Legendary witness newly printed in full by Lovewell; Cecilia occupies ff. 286r-288v.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: Second quarter of the fifteenth century

notes: The catalog associates the compiler's modified orthography with Hampshire, eLALME LP 5470.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: The South English Legendary Cecilia witness represented by L readings in Lovewell's Ashmole apparatus; the later-added Cecilia text occupies ff. 230v-233v.

notes: The codex core is late thirteenth or early fourteenth century with later additions; the Cecilia appendix is later than the core.

entities.label: Cambridge, Trinity College, MS R.3.25
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment South English Legendary witness represented by C readings in Lovewell's Ashmole apparatus; Cecilia occupies ff. 165r-167v.

entities.label: London, British Library, Cotton MS Cleopatra D IX
entities.description: A South English Legendary Cecilia witness newly printed in full by Lovewell; the current locus is ff. 155v-158v.

entities.label: London, British Library, Cotton MS Tiberius E VII, Part II
entities.description: The fire-damaged Northern Homily Cycle witness named by Lovewell's heading. DIMEV places Cecilia at ff. 271rb-273va and records illegible text and ending caused by fire damage; it cannot alone support Lovewell's complete 464-line stream.

holding.shelfmark: Cotton MS Tiberius E VII, Part II

date_statements.label: Part II date
date_statements.value: First half of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: 281 folios in two volumes; British Library catalog: Northern Homily Cycle ff. 101r-281r; DIMEV Cecilia locus ff. 271rb-273va
notes: Damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire; outer portions of leaves and many readings are lost or illegible.
The exact target locus comes from DIMEV v1.0. Horstmann's 1881 printed heading gives ff. 271-274b, an unresolved historical/current locator discrepancy.

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 4196
entities.description: The complete Northern Homily Cycle Cecilia witness at ff. 191r-193rb. Its folio numbers match Lovewell's internal milestones 192 and 193, and it supplies the complete text printed by Kölbing and the complete base of Horstmann's H/T presentation.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: Fourth quarter of the fourteenth century to first quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: 258 leaves plus flyleaves and fragments; Cecilia at ff. 191r-193rb

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Gg.2.6
entities.description: The unique paper witness of the Scottish Legendary. The current Cecilia locus is f. 354r through f. 360v; the next life, Lucy, begins on f. 361r.

notes: The historical catalog begins item 43 at f. 354a and the next item at f. 361a; Lovewell's internal milestones run 354b-360b.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Add. 3039
entities.description: A collateral South English Legendary Cecilia witness at ff. 43v-46v in DIMEV's current census; it was not a source identified by Lovewell.

entities.label: Cambridge, Fitzwilliam Museum, MS McClean 128
entities.description: A collateral fragment containing only the final twenty lines of the South English Legendary Cecilia on f. 8.

entities.label: Cambridge, Magdalene College, Pepys MS 2344
entities.description: A collateral South English Legendary Cecilia witness on pp. 508-514. It is not among the selected manuscripts currently exposed by the Pepys Digital Library.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: Second half of the fourteenth century

entities.label: London, British Library, Cotton MS Julius D IX
entities.description: A collateral South English Legendary Cecilia witness at ff. 187r-190r. DIMEV records a facsimile, but the British Library catalog reports the legacy complete digital object as currently unavailable.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: First half of the fifteenth century

entities.label: London, British Library, Egerton MS 2810
entities.description: A collateral South English Legendary Cecilia witness at ff. 114v-116r in DIMEV's current census.

entities.label: London, British Library, Stowe MS 949
entities.description: A collateral South English Legendary Cecilia witness at ff. 58r-61r. Lovewell says a Stowe copy was omitted, but gives the incompatible number 946.

entities.label: London, Lambeth Palace Library, MS 223
entities.description: A collateral South English Legendary Cecilia witness at ff. 221v-225r. Lovewell says this witness was omitted because its collation arrived too late.

entities.label: New Haven, Yale University, Takamiya MS 54
entities.description: A collateral South English Legendary Cecilia witness at ff. 166r-169v, now at the Beinecke Library and completely digitized.

notes: Yale describes the codex as substantially complete but missing leaves at the beginning, middle, and probably end.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS Ashmole 43
entities.description: A 580-canvas IIIF object cached as every width-1600 image plus provider manifest and inventory; Cecilia occupies canvases 386-392.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS Bodley 779
entities.description: A 672-canvas IIIF object cached as every width-1600 image plus provider manifest and inventory; Cecilia occupies canvases 591-596.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: A 496-canvas IIIF object cached as every width-1600 image plus provider manifest and inventory; Cecilia occupies canvases 476-482.

entities.label: Complete Trinity College facsimile of MS R.3.25
entities.description: A 586-canvas IIIF object cached as the complete published sequence; Cecilia occupies canvases 361-366.

entities.label: Complete Yale digital facsimile of Takamiya MS 54
entities.description: Yale's provider-declared complete 404-canvas digital object, cached as a 405-page generated PDF plus the IIIF manifest. PDF page 1 is a metadata sheet, so Cecilia at IIIF canvases 337-344 occupies PDF pages 338-345.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF and IIIF Presentation 3 manifest

entities.label: M. R. James unpublished description of CUL MS Gg.2.6A
entities.description: A historical unpublished description prepared by M. R. James in 1926-1930 and transcribed by Jayne Ringrose in 2002-2011. The cached one-page PDF describes Gg.2.6A as four manuscript leaves of paper, supplies the title '[Scottish Lives of Saints]', and directs readers to Gg.2.6 for contents. It is not a current full holding record, does not establish Cecilia's folio locus, and leaves the exact physical relationship between the Gg.2.6A unit and the manuscript now cataloged as Gg.2.6 for repository confirmation.
notes: The repository item is openly licensed CC BY 4.0; that license applies to this catalog-description object, not to separately supplied manuscript photography.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 records 4572 and 2803
entities.description: Versioned scholarly records for the South English Legendary and expanded Northern Homily Cycle Cecilia works, including identifiers, forms, subjects, incipits, explicits, exact witness loci, incompleteness, facsimile notes, and editions.

notes: qpdf --check completed successfully with recoverable warnings about linearization, shared page identifiers, hint-table membership, and an outline offset; pdfinfo and pdftotext both completed successfully.

No public facsimile was found. Ask for the whole manuscript and separately ask whether supplied images may be deposited in a public Git repository or used commercially.

editorial_practices.description: The encoding includes Lovewell's title page and comparative texts on pp. 72-102 but omits the monograph's preface, historical introduction, grammar, metrics, notes, glossaries, and bibliography.
editorial_practices.scope: CME00145 title page and printed pp. 72-102

editorial_practices.description: Lovewell organizes South English Legendary readings around Ashmole, prints Bodley and Cleopatra in full, gives Laud and Trinity variants, and constructs the Northern text through the H/T editorial tradition. Stowe and Lambeth arrived too late and were omitted.

Ashmole follows manuscript pointing through Furnivall; the Northern stream mainly follows Horstmann; Scottish punctuation combines Horstmann and Metcalfe; Cleopatra retains manuscript pointing; and the unpointed Bodley text receives modern punctuation.
Lovewell explicitly modernizes capitalization in the unpointed Bodley stream and combines Horstmann and Metcalfe as authorities for the Scottish stream.

Italic letters, often inside brackets, mark Lovewell's editorially supplied or emended letters and expansions. They are editorial intervention, not manuscript emphasis; CME HI/@REND='italic' preserves the printed convention.

The XML retains Lovewell's printed source headings, including historical or erroneous labels: Bodley 799 for Bodley 779, Tiberius ff. 271-274b over a composite H/T text, and Gg.II.6 ff. 364a-358b despite internal milestones 354b-360b.

evidence.citation: Bertha Ellen Lovewell, The Life of St. Cecilia from MS. Ashmole 43 and MS. Cotton Tiberius E. VII (1898).
evidence.locator: encoded title page = PDF page 7; preface; pp. 32-36; comparative texts pp. 72-102 = PDF pages 80-110; bibliography
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for edition identity and date, source grouping, named central texts, independent Latin comparison, library-made transcripts and collations, omitted Stowe/Lambeth material, emendation convention, punctuation and capitalization policies, source headings, and continuous version texts.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item lifeofstceciliaf00ceci, Library of Congress scan and metadata.
evidence.summary: Provider evidence for the complete 156-page scan, title, editor, publication year, publisher, file inventory, and absence of an item-level license field.

evidence.citation: F. J. Furnivall et al., Originals and Analogues of Some of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, Internet Archive item originalsanalogu00furnuoft.
evidence.summary: The complete public scan of the available bound item confirms the multi-part publication and index context but begins after the target Cecilia pages; it therefore supplies negative access evidence rather than a target facsimile.

evidence.citation: Eugen Kölbing, 'Zu Chaucer's Caecilien-Legende,' Englische Studien 1 (1877): 215-248.
evidence.locator: article pp. 215-248; De sancta Caecilia historia pp. 235-240 = PDF pages 247-252
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for Kölbing's article title and complete Harley 4196 Cecilia text, with folio milestones 191a-193 and a direct print precursor cited by Lovewell.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden, Neue Folge (1881), De sancta Cecilia historia, 159-164.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 159-164
evidence.summary: Primary evidence that the complete Northern text is headed from Tiberius ff. 271-274b while its apparatus distinguishes H and T; its wording and Harley folio positions explain Lovewell's complete Tiberius-labeled stream and internal milestones 192-193.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., Barbour's des schottischen Nationaldichters Legendensammlung, vol. 2 (1882), Cecilia, 164-172.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 164-172
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for Horstmann's Scottish Legendary Cecilia edition from Gg.2.6, one of Lovewell's stated punctuation and capitalization authorities.

evidence.citation: W. M. Metcalfe, ed., Legends of the Saints in the Scottish Dialect of the Fourteenth Century, vol. 2 (1890), Cecilia, 368-386.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 368-386 = PDF pages 391-409
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the unique-manuscript Scottish Cecilia text, its ending immediately before Lucy, and Lovewell's second punctuation and capitalization authority.

evidence.locator: De sancta Caecilia, printed pp. 771-776 = PDF pages 784-789

open_questions.question: At the individual-reading level, how did Lovewell derive her complete Tiberius-labeled text from Horstmann's H/T edition, and where does she depart from Harley, Tiberius, Kölbing, or Horstmann?
next_steps: Collate Lovewell pp. 92-102 line by line against Horstmann 1881 pp. 159-164 and Kölbing 1877 pp. 235-240.
Obtain complete color photography of Harley 4196 and Cotton Tiberius E VII.
Record every H-only, T-only, conjectural, and normalized reading without treating the printed heading as a direct-source assertion.

open_questions.question: Who made Lovewell's Bodley 779 and Cotton Cleopatra D IX transcripts and the Laud/Trinity collations, where are the working papers now, and did Lovewell alter them before publication?
next_steps: Search Lovewell, Albert S. Cook, Yale Studies in English, Bodleian, British Museum, and Trinity correspondence or archives.
Compare the printed streams against complete Bodley images and requested Cleopatra images.
Keep the unnamed working documents as explicit intermediaries until their identities or non-survival are established.

open_questions.question: Did Lovewell copy the Latin comparison from Grässe's 1846 first edition, 1850 second edition, another issue, or a mixed working source?
next_steps: Collate Lovewell's Latin text and punctuation against both complete Grässe scans.
Inspect bibliography wording, distinctive errors, and page-break correspondence.
Retain both cited editions as distinct sources until an exact copied issue is demonstrated.

open_questions.question: Can complete public or request-supplied facsimiles be obtained for the direct and collateral witnesses for which no complete public facsimile was verified in this review?
next_steps: Request each whole physical manuscript, including binding, covers, edges, flyleaves, blanks, and all leaves, while separately identifying the target loci.
Ask each provider whether supplied files may be deposited in a public Git repository and used in commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand output.
Record any quote, delivery format, license, and exact object coverage before adding redistributed files.

open_questions.question: Is there a public complete scan of the issued Chaucer Society Part II carrier containing Furnivall's Ashmole Cecilia on pp. 208-218?
next_steps: Search HathiTrust, Google Books, institutional digitization catalogs, and bound Chaucer Society issue records by series number 10 rather than only by collective title.
Do not describe the cached Internet Archive later-installment scan as target-bearing.
Use the complete Ashmole manuscript facsimile and Lovewell downstream text for verification until the exact carrier is found.

open_questions.question: Does Lovewell's repeated 'Stowe 946' refer by simple typographical error to the current DIMEV witness Stowe 949, or to a historical designation requiring a more complex shelfmark concordance?
next_steps: Consult the historical Stowe catalog and British Museum accession concordances.
Compare Lovewell's incipit, legend count, and former number 669 description against Stowe 949.
Retain Stowe 949 only as a collateral witness and do not claim it supplied printed variants because Lovewell says the Stowe material was omitted.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's software-worded root Unlicense intentionally apply to CME00145 XML markup and data?
next_steps: Seek a path-specific data or XML rights statement from the repository maintainer.
Do not broaden the software wording into a confirmed public-domain dedication for the XML until clarified.

open_questions.question: How should the current DIMEV Cecilia locus ff. 271rb-273va be reconciled with Horstmann's historical heading ff. 271-274b within the British Library catalog's broad Northern Homily Cycle range ff. 101r-281r?
next_steps: Obtain or inspect a current foliation map and complete color images for Cotton Tiberius E VII, Part II.
Compare column, recto-verso, historical, and current foliation across DIMEV, Horstmann, the holding catalog, and the physical leaves.
Continue attributing the exact modern locus to DIMEV rather than to the coarse British Library record.

open_questions.question: What is the current physical and catalog relationship between M. R. James's four-leaf unit Gg.2.6A and Cambridge University Library MS Gg.2.6, and does that unit intersect the Cecilia locus?
next_steps: Ask Cambridge University Library manuscripts staff for the current catalog record, collation, and any shelfmark or subunit concordance for Gg.2.6A.
Do not use the James description as evidence for Cecilia's ff. 354r-360v locus unless the repository confirms the physical mapping.
Record any current catalog response separately from the historical 1926-1930 description.
